description: An object made, chosen, or temporarily treated as something to play with is 'wilemo'. Children and adults may use toys, and an ordinary household object can enter the relation without changing what it is made from. 'wile' (play) names the activity; 'wilemo' is the thing handled within it.
articulatory_notes: Begin with rounded glide 'w' moving into high-front 'i'. Place the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'l' and release into stressed mid-front 'e', then close the lips for nasal 'm' and finish on mid-back rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The first two syllables repeat the sound of 'wile' (play). The plaything therefore has an audible place beside the activity.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A toy can be carved, sewn, improvised, mended, and handed on. Continued play gives it a history.

semantic_domains.physical: a made or selected thing handled as a plaything
